title: Design Inspiration Galleries & Award Sites
description: Where designers find visual inspiration: award programs, curated web galleries, and UI/UX pattern libraries.

entries.name: Awwwards
entries.summary: Awards program and gallery recognizing the best in web design, creativity, and development, judged by a global jury plus community votes.
entries.significance: The most prominent web-design award; its 'Site of the Day' and Honor scores are a widely watched barometer of cutting-edge web craft.
key_facts: Scores sites on design, usability, creativity, and content.
Grants Site of the Day (SOTD), Site of the Month, and Site of the Year honors.
Combines jury scoring with public voting.
Founded in 2009; based in Spain.

entries.name: CSS Design Awards
entries.summary: International web design and development award program recognizing outstanding sites with daily, monthly, and yearly honors.
entries.significance: A long-running peer alongside Awwwards for showcasing and rewarding high-end web design and front-end craft.
key_facts: Grants Website of the Day (WOTD) and UI/UX awards.
Uses a panel of judges scoring UI, UX, and innovation.
Maintains a searchable gallery of awarded sites.

entries.name: FWA (Favourite Website Awards)
entries.summary: One of the oldest web-design award programs, spotlighting cutting-edge and experimental interactive work since 2000.
entries.significance: Historically the premier showcase for immersive, technically ambitious sites, especially during the Flash era and later WebGL work.
key_facts: Founded in 2000 by Rob Ford.
Grants FWA of the Day (FOTD), of the Month, and of the Year.
Long associated with experimental, technology-forward interactive design.

entries.name: The Webby Awards
entries.summary: Long-established awards honoring excellence across websites, apps, video, podcasts, and digital campaigns.
entries.significance: The most mainstream and prestigious internet award, famous for its five-word acceptance speeches; presented by the IADAS.
key_facts: Founded in 1996.
Presented by the International Academy of Digital Arts and Sciences (IADAS).
Known for the five-word acceptance speech rule.
Covers a broad range of digital categories beyond web design.

entries.name: Godly
entries.summary: Highly selective gallery of standout websites, curated for bold, experimental, and technically impressive web design.
entries.significance: Prized for quality-over-quantity curation; a go-to for studio-grade, motion-rich, and award-level web inspiration.
key_facts: Curated by Rejiggle with an emphasis on quality over quantity.
Filterable by categories such as AI, Web3, portfolio, and e-commerce.
Highlights bold, innovative, and experimental web design.

entries.name: Land-book
entries.summary: Curated gallery of website and landing-page designs, organized by style, industry, colors, and type.
entries.significance: A well-established reference specifically for landing-page and marketing-site layouts.
key_facts: Focuses on landing pages and marketing sites.
Filterable by category, style, industry, colors, and typography.
One of the longer-running curated web galleries.

entries.name: Httpster
entries.summary: Curated web-design inspiration gallery collecting good-looking, current websites without heavy categorization.
entries.significance: A long-standing, personality-driven gallery known for a broad, trend-aware selection of contemporary sites.
key_facts: Curated collection of contemporary website designs.
Emphasizes visually strong, current sites.
Simple browsing without award scoring.
Founded in 2012 by Dominic Whittle; 3,100+ curated sites, organized by style and type.

entries.name: One Page Love
entries.summary: Gallery dedicated to single-page website designs, plus one-page templates, resources, and awards.
entries.significance: The definitive resource for one-page website inspiration and patterns.
key_facts: Founded by Rob Hope in 2008.
Specializes exclusively in single-page (one-page) websites.
Offers templates, hosting recommendations, and a One Page Award.

entries.name: SiteInspire
entries.summary: Curated showcase of web design filterable by style, type, subject, and platform.
entries.significance: A respected, long-running curated gallery favored for clean, well-organized browsing of quality web design.
key_facts: Filterable by style, site type, subject, and platform.
Emphasizes a selective, quality-focused editorial standard.
One of the earlier modern web-design galleries.

entries.name: Lapa Ninja
entries.summary: Gallery of landing-page designs organized by category, plus free design books and resources.
entries.significance: A popular free resource combining landing-page inspiration with curated learning materials.
key_facts: Focuses on landing-page design examples.
Categorized by industry and design element.
Also aggregates free design books and resources.
Launched in 2015; 7,300+ landing-page designs plus a library of free design books.

entries.name: Dribbble
entries.summary: Social platform where designers share 'shots' of their work, widely used for UI, branding, and illustration inspiration.
entries.significance: A dominant design community and portfolio network; hugely influential on UI trends, though sometimes criticized for prioritizing aesthetics over usability.
key_facts: Founded in 2009 by Dan Cederholm and Rich Thornett.
Designers post 'shots'; the name references basketball ('dribble').
Originally invite-only, which shaped its early exclusivity.
Used for UI, illustration, branding, and hiring.

entries.name: Behance
entries.summary: Adobe-owned platform for showcasing full creative project case studies across design, illustration, photography, and more.
entries.significance: One of the largest creative portfolio networks; favors in-depth project presentations over single shots.
key_facts: Founded in 2006 by Scott Belsky and Matias Corea.
Acquired by Adobe in 2012.
Structured around full project case studies rather than single images.
Integrated with Adobe Creative Cloud.

entries.name: Mobbin
entries.summary: Large searchable reference library of real mobile and web app screenshots and user flows.
entries.significance: A leading reference for studying real-world UI patterns and flows from shipping apps, widely used for competitive and pattern research.
key_facts: Describes itself as the world's largest UI/UX reference library.
Contains hundreds of thousands of fully searchable app screenshots.
Filterable by app, UI element, and flow pattern.
Includes a Figma plugin to copy reference designs.

entries.name: Screenlane
entries.summary: Library of web and mobile UI design inspiration organized by pattern, element, and category.
entries.significance: A convenient, filterable source of UI screenshots for specific components and patterns.
key_facts: Organizes screenshots by UI element and pattern.
Covers both mobile and web apps.
Offers a design-inspiration newsletter.
Rebranded from 'UI Movement' in 2020; daily mobile UI patterns delivered via site + newsletter.

entries.name: Page Flows
entries.summary: Reference library of recorded user flows (onboarding, checkout, etc.) from real apps and websites, captured as videos and screenshots.
entries.significance: Specializes in end-to-end UX flows rather than static screens, useful for studying interaction and onboarding design.
key_facts: Captures complete user flows as video recordings and screenshots.
Covers flows like onboarding, sign-up, checkout, and cancellation.
Includes both mobile and web experiences.

entries.name: UI Sources
entries.summary: Library of UI patterns and user flows from popular apps, aimed at product and UX research.
entries.significance: Another curated flow-and-pattern reference for benchmarking real product UX decisions.
key_facts: Collects UI patterns and flows from well-known apps.
Organized to support product and UX research.
Focuses on mobile app experiences.
iOS-focused; 700+ interactions and 2000+ screenshots. Note: uisources.com now presents as 'ScreensDesign'.

entries.name: Nicely Done
entries.summary: Curated library of screenshots and patterns from web and SaaS applications.
entries.significance: A focused reference for SaaS and web-app interface patterns.
key_facts: Concentrates on SaaS and web application UIs.
Organized by app and by UI pattern/section.
Used as a benchmark reference for product design.
Founded in 2016 by Bertrand; 230,000+ SaaS screens from 500+ apps.

entries.name: Refero
entries.summary: Searchable design reference library of screens and flows from real apps and websites.
entries.significance: A broad, searchable pattern reference for finding examples of specific screens and UI elements.
key_facts: Large collection of screenshots from real products.
Searchable by app, page type, and UI element.
Covers web and mobile.
125,000+ real product screens from 400+ web/iOS products; offers a Figma plugin and an MCP endpoint for AI agents.

entries.name: Collect UI
entries.summary: Daily-updated gallery of UI design inspiration curated from Dribbble shots and organized by category.
entries.significance: A convenient category-based collection of UI concepts, closely tied to the Daily UI challenge community.
key_facts: Aggregates UI inspiration curated from Dribbble.
Organized into categories (e.g., sign-up, checkout, 404).
Associated with the Daily UI email challenge.

entries.name: Pttrns
entries.summary: One of the earliest mobile UI pattern libraries, cataloging iOS and mobile app screens by pattern and category.
entries.significance: A pioneering mobile pattern reference that helped establish the 'UI pattern gallery' format for mobile design.
key_facts: Focuses primarily on mobile (notably iOS) UI patterns.
Categorized by screen type and interaction pattern.
One of the earliest dedicated mobile pattern libraries.
Covers iOS and Android patterns; membership-based curated library.

entries.name: Calltoidea
entries.summary: Gallery of web design and UI examples grouped by element and section (headers, pricing, footers, etc.).
entries.significance: Useful for finding many examples of a single component or page section quickly.
key_facts: Organized by UI element and page section.
Focuses on web interface examples.
Helps compare many variations of one component type.

entries.name: Minimal Gallery
entries.summary: Curated gallery showcasing websites with minimal, clean visual design.
entries.significance: A style-specific gallery for designers seeking restrained, minimalist web aesthetics.
key_facts: Curates websites in a minimalist visual style.
Emphasizes clean layouts, whitespace, and restraint.
Style-focused rather than award-based.
Created in 2013 by Piet Terheyden; daily-updated minimalist site inspiration.

entries.name: Brutalist Websites
entries.summary: Curated collection of websites in the web-brutalism style, cataloging raw, unconventional interface design.
entries.significance: The reference archive for the web brutalism movement, documenting a deliberately raw counter-aesthetic to polished UI.
key_facts: Dedicated to the web brutalism aesthetic.
Showcases raw, stark, and unconventional site designs.
Documents a specific design movement rather than general trends.

entries.name: Typewolf
entries.summary: Typographic inspiration site that showcases well-designed sites and identifies the fonts they use.
entries.significance: A leading resource for web typography inspiration and font identification, curated from a working designer's perspective.
key_facts: Curated by designer Jeremiah Shoaf.
Identifies the typefaces used in each featured site.
Publishes curated font lists and a weekly (Tuesday) roundup.
Independent of any single type foundry.

entries.name: Fonts In Use
entries.summary: Public archive documenting real-world typography, indexed by typeface, format, industry, and period.
entries.significance: An authoritative, crowd-documented record of how typefaces are used in practice across print and screen.
key_facts: Indexes real-world type usage by font, format, industry, and era.
Community-contributed with editorial curation.
Serves as both inspiration and type-history reference.

entries.name: Muzli
entries.summary: Design inspiration aggregator delivered as a browser new-tab feed, surfacing curated design content from across the web.
entries.significance: A widely installed daily design feed that pulls together inspiration from Dribbble, Behance, and design publications; owned by InVision.
key_facts: Delivered primarily as a browser new-tab extension.
Aggregates design news and inspiration from many sources.
Also publishes curated 'Muzli' collections and articles.
Acquired by InVision in July 2015; 800k+ users.

entries.name: Savee
entries.summary: Visual bookmarking and moodboarding tool for saving and organizing images, sites, and creative references.
entries.significance: A favored moodboarding platform among designers and art directors, seen as a successor to the discontinued Dropmark/VSCO-style curation tools.
key_facts: Lets users save images and links into visual collections/boards.
Popular for building moodboards and reference libraries.
Includes browser extensions for quick saving.
Ad-free with no algorithmic feed; 1M+ users. Primary domain is savee.com (savee.it is the legacy domain).

entries.name: Cosmos
entries.summary: Visual discovery and curation app for saving and organizing creative inspiration into collections ('clusters').
entries.significance: A fast-growing visual bookmarking platform positioned as a curated, algorithm-light alternative to Pinterest for creatives.
key_facts: Lets users collect visual inspiration into organized clusters.
Emphasizes creative and design-focused content discovery.
Available on web and mobile.
AI-powered auto-tagging by color/mood/style; named one of Apple's Apps of 2025.

entries.name: SaaS Landing Page
entries.summary: Curated gallery of SaaS website and landing-page designs, browsable by page section and category.
entries.significance: A targeted reference for SaaS marketing-site design, including section-level breakdowns (hero, pricing, features).
key_facts: Focuses specifically on SaaS landing pages.
Browsable by full pages and by individual sections.
Useful for marketing-site and conversion design.
